issues.title: Missing filter on transaction status
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status correctly as per the business rule.

issues.title: Incorrect aggregation
issues.line: SUM(t.amount) as lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders
issues.problem: The query calculates lifetime value and total orders without considering the status of the transaction.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value, COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id END) as total_orders

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic of the query.
issues.fix:
-- Calculate customer lifetime value and total completed orders
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id END) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;

review.corrected_sql:
-- Calculate customer lifetime value and total completed orders
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id END) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query had issues with filtering transaction status and calculating lifetime value correctly. Comments were also added for readability.
